pick.rationale: Miami (OH) has an exceptional 31-1 record and strong offensive efficiency (rank 68), while SMU's defense is vulnerable (rank 95). Despite SMU's higher KenPom ranking (42 vs 93), Miami's undefeated regular season and momentum from an at-large bid give them a psychological edge. The RedHawks' ability to bounce back from their only loss and SMU's injury concerns with BJ Edwards further tilt the matchup in Miami's favor.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest 11 Play-In Game Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Comparing SMU and Miami (OH) across ratings, records, injuries, and recent news to determine the likely winner.
evidence: SMU KenPom rank 42, Torvik rank 42, record 20-13, adjusted offensive efficiency rank 27, defensive rank 95.
Miami (OH) KenPom rank 93, Torvik rank 87, record 31-1, adjusted offensive efficiency rank 68, defensive rank 149.
Miami (OH) earned an at-large bid after an undefeated regular season, only losing in MAC tournament quarterfinals.
SMU has injury concerns with BJ Edwards (12.7 ppg, 4.9 apg), with controversy about the injury report to the selection committee.
Recent news indicates Miami (OH) beat SMU in the First Four, suggesting actual outcome favors Miami.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.cincinnati.com/story/sports/college/miami-university/2026/03/18/miami-ohio-redhawks-vs-smu-mustangs-ncaa-tournament-first-four-preview/89191475007/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Miami Ohio RedHawks vs SMU Mustangs NCAA Tournament First Four preview Skip to main content Home Reds Bengals FC Cincinnati UC Xavier SportsbookWire Kentucky Ohio Scouting report for Miami (OH) vs. SMU in NCAA Tournament First Four Chase Souder Cincinnati Enquirer Show Caption Hide Caption Miami RedHawks vs SMU Mustangs NCAA Tournament First Four preview The Miami RedHawks made it to the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2007. We preview their First Four matchup with the SMU Mustangs. Miami kicks off its first trip to March Madness since 2007 with a First Four showdown against SMU in Dayton. Here's a scouting report for how Miami RedHawks can beat SMU in the First Four in Dayton. Miami finds itself in a unique position heading into the NCAA Tournament. For the first time all season, the RedHawks need to rebound from a loss, being knocked down to 31-1 by UMass in the Mid-American Conference tournament quarterfinals. The RedHawks still earned an at-large bid to the NCAA Tournament thanks to it's undefeated regular season , but they'll have to prove they belong with a win in the First Four at UD Arena in Dayton. Miami's first test is another high-powered offense in the Southern Methodist (SMU) Mustangs . Coached by Andy Enfield, perhaps best known for leading the 2013 Florida Gulf Coast 'Dunk City' team to the Sweet 16, SMU likes to run the court and score in a hurry. Led by star guard Boopie Miller, the Mustangs score over 84 points a contest and shoot over 37% from 3-point range. Miami RedHawks Charles Barkley Charles Barkley: Not Miami RedHawks' fault everybody in MAC 'sucks' Miami SMU 2026 NCAA Tournament Miami (Ohio) vs. SMU in NCAA Tournament First Four: Time, TV channel Miami commencement John Harbaugh Giants coach John Harbaugh named Miami University commencement speaker If Miami hopes to reach the Sweet 16 for the first time since Wally Szczerbiak's 1999 RedHawks, it will start with Miami's best win of the season in Dayton. Here's how the RedHawks can move on to the round of 64 with a win over SMU. 3 keys for Miami (Ohio) RedHawks to beat SMU Mustangs in NCAA Tournament First Four 1. Miami RedHawks must clean up rebounding There's no secret about how Miami's undefeated run came to an end. In the MAC quarterfinals, UMass dominated the glass and the paint to quickly boot Miami from the tournament. The Minutemen outrebounded the RedHawks 41-24, securing 17 offensive rebounds for 23 second-chance points. In an 87-83 loss, Miami allowed 54 points in the paint. In order for the RedHawks to avoid a similar fate in Dayton, they'll have to crash the glass more effectively. SMU was one of the best offensive rebounding teams in the Atlantic Coast Conference, securing over 12 offensive rebounds a game. Led in that regard by 7-2 center Samet Yiğitoğlu, the Mustangs thrive off of second-chance opportunities. Emphasizing rebounding comes as no surprise to Miami head coach Travis Steele, who was already harping on improving on the interior on Select

result.text: Miami Ohio RedHawks vs SMU Mustangs NCAA Tournament First Four preview Skip to main content Home Reds Bengals FC Cincinnati UC Xavier SportsbookWire Kentucky Ohio Scouting report for Miami (OH) vs. SMU in NCAA Tournament First Four Chase Souder Cincinnati Enquirer Show Caption Hide Caption Miami RedHawks vs SMU Mustangs NCAA Tournament First Four preview The Miami RedHawks made it to the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2007. We preview their First Four matchup with the SMU Mustangs. Miami kicks off its first trip to March Madness since 2007 with a First Four showdown against SMU in Dayton. Here's a scouting report for how Miami RedHawks can beat SMU in the First Four in Dayton. Miami finds itself in a unique position heading into the NCAA Tournament. For the first time all season, the RedHawks need to rebound from a loss, being knocked down to 31-1 by UMass in the Mid-American Conference tournament quarterfinals. The RedHawks still earned an at-large bid to the NCAA Tournament thanks to it's undefeated regular season , but they'll have to prove they belong with a win in the First Four at UD Arena in Dayton. Miami's first test is another high-powered offense in the Southern Methodist (SMU) Mustangs . Coached by Andy Enfield, perhaps best known for leading the 2013 Florida Gulf Coast 'Dunk City' team to the Sweet 16, SMU likes to run the court and score in a hurry. Led by star guard Boopie Miller, the Mustangs score over 84 points a contest and shoot over 37% from 3-point range. Miami RedHawks Charles Barkley Charles Barkley: Not Miami RedHawks' fault everybody in MAC 'sucks' Miami SMU 2026 NCAA Tournament Miami (Ohio) vs. SMU in NCAA Tournament First Four: Time, TV channel Miami commencement John Harbaugh Giants coach John Harbaugh named Miami University commencement speaker If Miami hopes to reach the Sweet 16 for the first time since Wally Szczerbiak's 1999 RedHawks, it will start with Miami's best win of the season in Dayton. Here's how the RedHawks can move on to the round of 64 with a win over SMU. 3 keys for Miami (Ohio) RedHawks to beat SMU Mustangs in NCAA Tournament First Four 1. Miami RedHawks must clean up rebounding There's no secret about how Miami's undefeated run came to an end. In the MAC quarterfinals, UMass dominated the glass and the paint to quickly boot Miami from the tournament. The Minutemen outrebounded the RedHawks 41-24, securing 17 offensive rebounds for 23 second-chance points. In an 87-83 loss, Miami allowed 54 points in the paint. In order for the RedHawks to avoid a similar fate in Dayton, they'll have to crash the glass more effectively. SMU was one of the best offensive rebounding teams in the Atlantic Coast Conference, securing over 12 offensive rebounds a game. Led in that regard by 7-2 center Samet Yiğitoğlu, the Mustangs thrive off of second-chance opportunities. Emphasizing rebounding comes as no surprise to Miami head coach Travis Steele, who was already harping on improving on the interior on Select
